summary.intent: Perform a comprehensive multi-domain code review of the optimize-anything project using parallel specialized agents, then autonomously fix all identified issues using a ralph-loop.
summary.outcome: 5 parallel review agents completed across source code, tests, docs, plugin, and architecture domains; a ralph-loop then addressed all critical/moderate issues across 4 commits, raising test count from 252 to 258 and pushing to remote.

repo: plugin.json declared 7 slash commands but only commands/optimize.md existed — 6 had no filesystem backing
No LICENSE file existed despite pyproject.toml declaring MIT license
AGENTS.md was missing the analyze subcommand and two source modules (llm_judge.py, spec_loader.py)
evaluator-cookbook.md documented only 2 of 3 evaluator types — omitted LLM judge (--judge-model)
.entire/ directory existed at project root but was not in .gitignore
CLAUDE.md was identified as the most accurate reference doc; other docs had drifted from actual CLI behavior
The test suite had no coverage for consecutive_smoke_gate.py at all
score_check.py hardcodes ['bash', ...] for all evaluators, constraining the regression suite to bash-only scripts

code.finding: Budget sentinel bug: `if args.budget == 100` cannot distinguish user-explicit --budget 100 from argparse default; spec file silently overrides. Fixed by changing argparse default to None and applying 100 fallback after spec merging.
subprocess.run had no timeout argument — CI gate could hang indefinitely if evaluator stalls. Fixed by adding timeout=30 and catching TimeoutExpired.

Inconsistent error contracts in same module: llm_judge_evaluator returns (0.0, {'error': ...}) tuples but analyze_for_dimensions (line 360) raises RuntimeError — callers must handle incompatible patterns.
Dual validation logic: validate_evaluator_payload (preflight, lines 125-142) and _parse_evaluator_result (runtime, lines 145-171) duplicate the same checks and can silently diverge.
Duplicate quality_dimension names silently accepted — _compute_weighted_score would double-count a dimension. Fixed by adding seen_names set check in _normalize_quality_dimensions.
Redundant except (ValueError, Exception) — ValueError is a subclass of Exception making it dead code; swallows programming errors. Fixed to except json.JSONDecodeError.
--evaluator-command was appended before other flags in subprocess cmd list, contradicting documented nargs='+' greedy-parsing contract. Moved to last position.
Code fence stripping logic duplicated inline in _parse_judge_response (lines 175-182) vs named helper _strip_code_fences (lines 290-298). Fixed to call shared helper.
_load_spec_if_provided has three-state return type dict | None | False where False signals error — non-idiomatic sentinel pattern that type checkers cannot catch.
_extract_failure_metric_signal uses fragile string-token matching on metric names (failure_tokens tuple) with no tests — metric named 'detail_of_failure_modes' would spuriously trigger.

workflow: Domain-specialized parallel agents (5 agents by expertise domain) are more token-efficient than file-parallel agents — each reads only its domain slice (~1,500-4,200 lines) instead of the full 8K-line codebase
Ralph loop PROMPT.md design with batched validation gates (pytest after each batch) enables self-correcting iterative fixes with machine-verifiable completion criteria
The ralph-loop plugin had a shell interpretation bug: the ``` ! ``` code block syntax caused shells to interpret <promise> tags in arguments; fix is to use Bash() function call syntax instead
Ralph loop shebang portability: #!/bin/bash should be #!/usr/bin/env bash in plugin scripts

When patching imports inside function bodies in tests, the patch target must match where the name is looked up at call time (e.g., gepa.optimize_anything.optimize_anything not optimize_anything.cli.optimize_anything)

friction: Two API 500 internal server errors interrupted the workflow before the ralph-loop launched

Ralph loop /ralph-loop skill was broken due to shell interpretation of <promise> tags in the command file — required reading the GitHub issue and manually patching the plugin cache file before proceeding
Budget CLI test required iteration: initial patch target for gepa import was wrong (needed gepa.optimize_anything.optimize_anything, not the local module path)
open_items: consecutive_smoke_gate.py has zero test coverage — no tests for _run_pass, parse_args, or main
score_check.py hardcodes ['bash', path] — only bash evaluators supported in regression suite; needs schema extension for Python/HTTP evaluators
Architecture duplication deferred: ~60 lines of evaluator-selection logic duplicated between _cmd_optimize and _cmd_score; intake loading pattern repeated 3x in CLI handlers
spec_loader.py has no mutual exclusion validation for conflicting evaluator.command + evaluator.url in same spec file
_load_spec_if_provided three-state return type (dict | None | False) not refactored — still non-idiomatic
Plugin skill directories (generate-evaluator, optimization-guide) still lack examples/ subdirectories with sample scripts
plugin.json author field is plain string rather than structured object {name, url}
